questions.question: By how much can a solo founder with 50-200 customers reduce churn in 90 days with a proactive retention system?
questions.explanation: A solo founder with 50-200 customers can reduce churn by 25-40% in 90 days with a proactive system that requires only 2-3 hours per week. The key is shifting from reactive firefighting to systematic detection, intervention, and escalation.

questions.question: What are the four components of a proactive retention system?

options.text: Detect risk early, intervene automatically, escalate strategically, learn continuously

questions.explanation: The four components are: (1) Detect risk early via health scores and signal tracking, (2) Intervene automatically with reactivation sequences and feature nudges, (3) Escalate strategically with save plays for high-value accounts, and (4) Learn continuously through monthly retrospectives and playbook updates.

questions.question: What does the Retention Paradox state?

options.text: The more customers you have, the more churn you experience
The less time you spend on retention, the better your retention gets — because systems scale, panic doesn't
Retention is more expensive than acquisition
High-paying customers churn less than low-paying customers

questions.explanation: The Retention Paradox: the less time you spend on retention (through better systems), the better your retention gets. Systems scale predictably, while reactive panic-based retention doesn't. A 2-3 hour/week systematic approach beats 20 hours of firefighting.

questions.question: The retention playbook should be built once and never modified after the initial 14-day implementation sprint.
questions.explanation: False. The playbook is a living system with monthly retrospectives to learn from churn patterns and quarterly updates to refine triggers, scripts, and automation recipes. Continuous iteration is what separates proactive retention from one-time projects.

questions.question: In the 14-day retention system build, what is the focus of Days 1-3 (Foundation)?

options.text: Setting up health scoring, churn signal tracking, and benchmark analysis

questions.explanation: Days 1-3 focus on the foundation: building your health scoring spreadsheet, setting up the 7-signal tracker with alerts, calculating your baseline churn rate and NRR, running a cohort analysis, and comparing against SMB SaaS benchmarks.

questions.question: Create your 14-day retention system build plan. For each phase (Foundation Days 1-3, Automation Days 4-7, Playbooks Days 8-10, Launch Days 11-14), list the specific deliverables you will produce and the tools you will use.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response covers all four phases with specific, product-relevant deliverables (health scoring setup, automation recipes, save play scripts, measurement dashboard), names specific tools they will use, and prioritizes based on their biggest retention gap.
